sections.paragraph:
ἀδιακρίτως adv. (Anecd. Gr. 352; Proclus on Pla., Crat. p. 91, 13; schol. on Apollon. Rhod. 2, 62; Philo, Fgm. 105 Harris [1886]; TestZeb 7:2; POxy 715, 36 [131 a.d.]=Wilcken, APF 4, 1908, 254) without wavering IRo ins; IPhld ins (s. ἀδιάκριτος).

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 19
